description: HADES-SA telemetry decode regression: real CRC-OK off-air frames (captured from 2026-04-17_14_54_53_HADES-SA.iq.wav via the headless StreamingPipeline decoder), pinned to expected fields. This is the off-air guard for the schema-v2 features that ONLY HADES exercises in a real recording: linear scale+offset, special (sentinel) values, units, enum, a visible repeat array (time_series), the empty-BBS detection (type 15: the message store is always empty off-air, so it is rendered as a single 'empty (No data)' marker; the group/str/repeat mechanics are covered synthetically), AND the type-1 power frame's cross-byte nibble-packing + nonlinear calibration (LE u2 ADC words -> bitwise/shift expr: vbus1/vbat1 *1.4, vcpu reciprocal 1210*4096/x, vbus2/3+vbat2 *4, ibat signed 12-bit). The power calibration mirrors the satnogs-decoders hadesr.ksy `_dec` instances (same AMSAT-EA bus as HADES-SA, identical seq: sclock u4 + spa-spd u1 + spi u2 + 7 ADC u2 words); LE word reads were confirmed correct by producing physically-sane voltages (~3.9-4.3 V, vcpu ~2.8 V) and a small signed ibat, vs. garbage from a clean MSB-first 12-bit read. HADES dispatch is on the 4-bit `type` (high nibble of byte 0). Type-4 (power_ranges) and type-9 (ext_power INA) are not yet pinned (type 4 = same approach, follow-up; type 9 spec values are 'raw').

frames.feature: cross-byte nibble-pack + nonlinear cal (bitwise/shift expr over LE ADC words)
enum + visible repeat array (vbat1: non-temp variable → raw counts, samples named by T-minus minute)

frames.note: EMPTY-BBS frame: the on-board message store is empty, so the firmware fills it with filler ('-' dashes + the repeated 'No data' sentinel), NOT real BBS records. Every type-15 BBS frame in every HADES-SA recording we have (2026-04-17/06-02/06-07/06-18) is this identical filler — no real-callsign BBS frame exists off-air. The parser detects the empty store and emits a single 'bbs = empty (No data)' marker instead of slicing the filler into 15 meaningless callsign/message/count fields. The group/str/repeat parse mechanics are still covered off this path by the synthetic Hades_Type15_Bbs_GroupRepeatReproducesExplicitNames unit test.
